query: Can you recommend some literature that focuses on dependency-based models for relation extraction, especially those that utilize dependency parsing to capture non-local syntactic relations?
criteria: dependency parsing for non-local syntactic relations in relation extraction
capturing long-distance dependencies in relation extraction using dependency parsing

title.value: A Composite Kernel to Extract Relations between Entities with both Flat and Structured Features

recommendation_reason.value: significantly outperforms previous two dependency tree kernels for relation extraction

supported_research_question.value: This paper proposes a novel composite kernel for relation extraction.

method.value: The composite kernel consists of two individual kernels: an entity kernel that allows for entity-related features and a convolution parse tree kernel that models syntactic information of relation examples.

main_conclusion.value: our method outperforms the previous best-reported methods and significantly outperforms previous two dependency tree kernels for relation extraction

limitations.value: It also shows without the need for extensive feature engineering the composite kernel can not only capture most of the flat features used in the previous work but also exploit the useful syntactic structure features effectively.

relevance_strength.value: significantly outperforms previous two dependency tree kernels for relation extraction

title.value: TEG-REP: A Corpus of Textual Entailment Graphs based on Relation Extraction Patterns

recommendation_reason.value: Dependency parse trees have become a popular source for discovering extraction patterns

supported_research_question.value: which encode the grammatical relations among the phrases that jointly express relation instances

method.value: We propose a new approach to structuring extraction patterns by utilizing entailment graphs

data_or_scenario.value: present a novel resource of gold-standard entailment graphs based on a set of patterns automatically acquired using distant supervision

main_conclusion.value: present statistics of the resource as well as an analysis of inference types underlying the entailment decisions

limitations.value: Among these patterns, some are semantically equivalent, but differ in their morphological, lexical-semantic or syntactic form.

relevance_strength.value: Dependency parse trees have become a popular source for discovering extraction patterns

title.value: Discovery of Dependency Tree Patterns for Relation Extraction

recommendation_reason.value: In this paper, we try to solve the problem of relation extraction by discovering dependency tree patterns (a pattern is an embedded sub dependency tree indicating a relation instance).

supported_research_question.value: Relation extraction is to identify the relations between pairs of named entities.

method.value: Our approach is to find an optimal rule (pattern) set automatically based on the proposed dependency tree pattern mining algorithm.

data_or_scenario.value: The experimental results show that the extracted patterns can achieve a high precision and a reasonable recall rate when used as rules to extract relation instances.

main_conclusion.value: The experimental results show that the extracted patterns can achieve a high precision and a reasonable recall rate when used as rules to extract relation instances.

limitations.value: Agichtein (2000) proposed Snowball which improved DIPRE by introducing the notion of match degree, where only sentences, whose match degrees are higher than a pre-defined threshold, are used to generate new rules.

relevance_strength.value: discovering dependency tree patterns (a pattern is an embedded sub dependency tree indicating a relation instance)
